name: City & County
description_short: City & County Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, and personal loans with a focus on member benefits over shareholder profits.
description_long:
City & County Credit Union (CCCU) operates as a member-owned financial cooperative, distinguishing itself from traditional banks through its not-for-profit structure. The organization positions itself around the principle that member wins are organizational wins, with profits reinvested into the membership rather than distributed to shareholders. The credit union maintains a physical and digital presence, offering branch locations alongside online banking services at cccu.com.

CCCU provides a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including free checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, and mortgage services. Their current promotional rates include auto loans as low as 4.49% APR, mortgages at 6.569% APR (30-year fixed), credit cards starting at 11.50% APR, and 9-month CDs offering up to 3.80% APY. The organization emphasizes mortgage lending with closing cost assistance up to $1,000 and positions auto financing as a primary product category.

What distinguishes CCCU is its explicit member-owner governance model, where members have voting rights to shape organizational priorities. The credit union differentiates itself through fee-free checking accounts, transparent pricing with no "sneaky fees," and a stated commitment to member service over sales quotas. They offer supplementary financial education including free credit report reviews, online classes and workshops on budgeting and home buying, and published content through their blog ("The Pink Pages") and financial wellness guides.

As a credit union, CCCU operates within the cooperative banking framework and emphasizes member advocacy and financial education. However, the website provides limited detail on membership requirements, whether membership is restricted by geography or employer, loan approval speed, or specific underwriting standards. The organization appears to compete primarily on rates and member-focused philosophy rather than specialized lending products or technology innovation.
services: Home buying guides and educational resources
pros: Fee-free checking accounts with straightforward features
Member-owner governance structure with voting rights on organizational priorities
Competitive rates including auto loans as low as 4.49% APR and mortgages at 6.569% APR
Mortgage closing cost assistance up to $1,000
Free financial education including credit report reviews, workshops, and online guides
Profits returned to members through lower fees and better rates rather than shareholder distributions
No sales quotas; stated focus on helping members reach goals rather than pushing products
cons: Website does not clarify membership eligibility criteria or whether membership is geographically or occupationally restricted
Limited transparency on loan approval timelines or underwriting standards
No mention of digital-only or neobanking features; appears traditional branch-dependent
No information on overdraft fees, ATM network coverage, or other standard account fees despite "fee-free" positioning
Credit card APR starts at 11.50%, which is mid-to-high range compared to competitive issuers
best_for: Homebuyers seeking mortgage assistance and closing cost relief
Auto buyers wanting competitive rates without sales-focused pressure
Financially-conscious consumers who value transparency and member-owner governance
Individuals interested in free financial education and credit counseling services
diagnosis: City & County Credit Union is best for consumers who prioritize member governance, transparent pricing, and community-focused banking over cutting-edge digital features. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is not clearly defined on their public website, requiring potential members to contact the organization directly to confirm access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
